raw_response:
I agree with the consensus that option B, egg yolk, correctly identifies the food richest in cholesterol. One additional point worth noting is that while coconut oil itself has negligible cholesterol content, many commercial products labeled "coconut oil" often undergo refining processes involving chemicals or solvents that do not remove all traces of residual cholesterol. However, since none of our original choices included modified versions of these ingredients, we must stick strictly to unprocessed forms. Therefore, my agreement remains firm with the selection of B: Egg Yolk. 

Answer Choice: B.
